title: Cheilanthes cheiloglyphis
taxonomy: Cheilanthes cheiloglyphis is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It is commonly known as the 'Hair fern'. The genus Cheilanthes belongs to the order Polypodiales and is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Cheilanthes cheiloglyphis specifically is found in the tropical regions of Central and South America, growing on rocks and trees. This plant is classified into the kingdom Plantae, further categorized into the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns.
uses: Cheilanthes cheiloglyphis has been found to have various medicinal properties, with its leaves being traditionally used to treat fever and inflammation. The plant's leaves are believed to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of certain health conditions. In traditional medicine, the plant is also used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. Cheilanthes cheiloglyphis is also thought to possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its effectiveness in treating various health conditions.
